Retainer Agreement
ERISA Counsel Engagement

Counsel Firm

{{ref}}
§ 01Parties

This Retainer Agreement is entered into between:

{{companyName}} (represented by {{ownerName}})
{{counselFirm}}
Independence note: Forhemit Stewardship Management Co. is not a party to this agreement. This preserves the independence of ERISA counsel's advice to the company and its ESOP plan participants.
§ 02Scope of Engagement

The Company retains Counsel to provide legal advice and representation in connection with the establishment and implementation of an Employee Stock Ownership Plan, including:

A.Plan document review. Drafting and reviewing the ESOP plan document, trust agreement, and related instruments to ensure compliance with ERISA and the Internal Revenue Code.
B.ERISA compliance. Advising on fiduciary duty, prohibited transaction rules, and Department of Labor requirements applicable to the ESOP transaction.
C.Fiduciary duty analysis. Advising the Company's board of directors and plan fiduciaries on their obligations, including the duty of loyalty, prudence, and diversification.
D.DOL interaction. Assisting with any Department of Labor inquiries, investigations, or advisory opinions related to the ESOP transaction.
E.Tax qualification. Advising on IRC §401(a), §4975, and §1042 requirements to ensure the plan qualifies for favorable tax treatment.
§ 03Independence Requirement

Counsel's professional obligation is to the Company and its ESOP plan participants. Counsel must exercise independent professional judgment at all times.

A.Counsel's duty of loyalty runs to the Company and the plan participants, not to Forhemit, the trustee, the lender, or any other deal party.
B.Counsel must not allow the interests of any third party — including Forhemit — to compromise independent legal advice to the Company.
C.Counsel has no obligation to Forhemit and Forhemit has no authority to direct Counsel's work product or legal opinions.
§ 04Fee Structure
{{hourlyRate}}
{{retainerAmount}}
Monthly, against retainer

The retainer is payable upon execution of this agreement. Fees are billed against the retainer on a monthly basis. If fees exceed the retainer amount, the Company will be invoiced for the difference. Unused retainer amounts are refundable upon termination, less any outstanding fees.

§ 05Conflict Disclosure

Counsel represents and warrants that, to the best of its knowledge after reasonable inquiry:

A.Counsel has no existing attorney-client relationship with Forhemit Stewardship Management Co., the proposed ESOP trustee, or the proposed SBA lender that would impair Counsel's independent advice to the Company.
B.Counsel has no financial interest in the outcome of the ESOP transaction beyond the fees described in this agreement.
C.If any conflict of interest arises during the engagement, Counsel will promptly disclose it to the Company and, if necessary, withdraw from the engagement.
§ 06Communication

Counsel will communicate directly with the Company regarding all matters in this engagement.

A.Forhemit may be copied on non-privileged communications for coordination purposes. Privileged communications must never be shared with Forhemit or other deal parties without the Company's express written consent.
B.Counsel will provide regular status updates to the Company at mutually agreed intervals, and promptly advise of any material issues or developments.
§ 07Term

This engagement begins on the date of execution and continues through the closing of the ESOP transaction, or the termination of the transaction, whichever occurs first.

§ 08Termination

Either party may terminate this agreement with fifteen (15) days' written notice to the other party. Upon termination:

A.All earned and accrued fees through the termination date remain due and payable.
B.Counsel will return all Company files and materials within ten (10) business days of termination.
C.Any unused retainer balance will be refunded within thirty (30) days.
§ 09Governing Law

This agreement is governed by the laws of the State of {{governingState}}, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions.

§ 10Contact

For questions about this engagement:

deals@forhemit.com
